title: Fold-safe empirical pit-hazard tables
summary: Pit timing is a discrete-time hazard problem, and public survival references suggest flexible discrete-time models often beat simpler continuous assumptions.
rationale: Pit timing is a discrete-time hazard problem, and public survival references suggest flexible discrete-time models often beat simpler continuous assumptions. A shrinkage hazard table can learn real pit windows from data instead of relying on fixed 24/33/43-lap constants.
implementation_hint: Create out-of-fold hazard features for binned `TyreLife`, `LapsRemaining_Est`, `Compound`, `Stint`, `RaceProgress`, and wet/slick regime. Smooth sparse cells hierarchically back to coarser bins and feed both raw hazards and hazard-minus-global-baseline features into the main tabular model.
expected_effect: Better local ranking around true pit windows, especially where the current handcrafted life constants are too rigid by race phase or compound.
risk: Easy to leak if tables are fit on full data; wet compounds and rare late-race bins need aggressive shrinkage.
